source_packet.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
source_packet.audience: Readers who understand dot-product attention and want the paper's formulation, implementation, and empirical findings.
source_packet.central_question: Can absolute position be encoded in query and key representations while their inner product depends explicitly on relative position?

formula_code_links.role: implements the 2D quarter-turn within every feature pair
takes the inner product after both query and key are rotated

example.label: One CAIL2019-SCM triple through the official fine-tuning script
example.input: A legal case A is paired once with candidate B and once with candidate C.
example.output: The two sigmoid scores are compared and contribute one correct/incorrect evaluation decision.

nodes.label: Load one A/B/C case
nodes.detail: The label determines which candidate is the positive continuation for case A.

nodes.label: Tokenize A+B and A+C
nodes.detail: The generator emits two paired sequences with labels 1 and 0, then pads the batch.

nodes.label: Run the RoFormer encoder
nodes.detail: build_transformer_model loads model='roformer' before the task head.

nodes.label: Construct cos/sin phases
nodes.detail: The official README repeats the position frequencies across adjacent feature pairs.

nodes.label: Rotate query and key pairs
nodes.detail: Negate-and-swap companions implement q·cos + q⊥·sin and the same operation for k.

nodes.label: Compute query-key scores
nodes.detail: The ordinary einsum now contains the relative-position identity from Eq. 16.

nodes.label: Pool and score each pair
nodes.detail: Global average pooling and a sigmoid head produce one score for A+B and one for A+C.

nodes.label: Compare the pair scores
nodes.detail: Evaluation counts the example correct when the positive pair score exceeds the negative pair score.

nodes.label: Retain the best validation epoch
nodes.detail: The callback saves weights only when validation accuracy improves, then reports test accuracy.

experiment_pipeline.label: Fine-tune by task
experiment_pipeline.detail: translation, GLUE, and long-text classification settings differ

experiment_pipeline.label: Read metric in context
experiment_pipeline.detail: BLEU, task scores, accuracy, and loss curves are not interchangeable

equation_coverage.intent: Establish the additive construction used by learned and sinusoidal absolute position encodings.

equation_coverage.thread_stage: additive and decomposed relative position encoding
equation_coverage.intent: Show how prior methods insert relative terms into the expanded attention score.
State the functional requirement that the inner product depend on m−n.
Solve the functional requirement in the complex plane and make the rotation geometric.
Pair feature dimensions and use orthogonality to obtain R at n−m in the attention score.
Show how rotation can be inserted without changing the kernelized attention form.
Prove intermediate identities and derive the elementwise implementation.
equation_coverage.fold_reason: These equations support the derivation and implementation but repeat the same pairwise rotation operation; the website keeps the official code mapping and appendix link instead of thirteen near-duplicate animations.

equation_coverage.intent: Decompose the rotary score by frequency and bound how it changes with relative distance.

finding_coverage.question: Does the RoFormer architecture retain translation quality?
finding_coverage.takeaway: Table 1 reports 27.5 for RoFormer and 27.3 for Transformer-base.

finding_coverage.question: Does RoPE change the shown pre-training optimization trajectory?
finding_coverage.setting: BookCorpus + Wikipedia MLM and Performer comparisons
finding_coverage.takeaway: Figure 3 reports faster convergence for RoFormer and for Performer equipped with RoPE; the curves are not digitized here.

finding_coverage.question: Is RoFormer uniformly better across GLUE tasks?
finding_coverage.setting: Six GLUE datasets under the paper's pre-training setup
finding_coverage.takeaway: Table 2 is mixed: RoFormer is higher on MRPC, STS-B, and QQP, but lower on SST-2, QNLI, and MNLI in the reported setup.

finding_coverage.question: Does a longer sequence setting help on long legal text?
finding_coverage.takeaway: Table 5 reports 68.29 at length 512 and 69.79 at length 1024; sequential training stages and other confounds remain relevant.

finding_coverage.question: How does the rotary score behave as relative distance grows?
finding_coverage.metric: upper bound on the summed rotary contribution
finding_coverage.takeaway: The paper derives a distance-dependent bound and presents Figure 2 as evidence of long-term decay.

formula_showcase.title: Eqs. 14–16 · absolute rotations, relative score
formula_showcase.focus: R_m and R_n enter separately, then orthogonality collapses their product to R_{n−m}.

formula_showcase.title: Eqs. 20–34 · parallel 2D rotations
formula_showcase.focus: Each adjacent coordinate pair uses the same position m with its own frequency θ_j.

formula_showcase.title: Eqs. 35–37 · distance-dependent analysis
formula_showcase.focus: The schematic motion explains the direction of the derived bound without digitizing Figure 2.

paper_excerpts.text: The paper narrates a specific equation lineage: the Transformer adds learned or sinusoidal absolute position before projection (Eqs. 3-4); Shaw et al. add a learned relative key term (Eq. 5); Transformer-XL decomposes content and relative-position interactions (Eqs. 6-7); T5, TUPE, and DeBERTa retain, remove, or disentangle terms (Eqs. 8-10). The authors' shared diagnosis is that prior work modifies an additive decomposition, motivating a multiplicative construction.
Equation 11 formulates the relative-position requirement. Equations 14-16 give the rotary solution and show that the query-key inner product contains R at relative position n minus m.

Table 1 reports 27.5 BLEU for RoFormer and 27.3 for Transformer-base on WMT 2014 English-to-German. Figure 3 reports faster convergence in the shown language-model pre-training and Performer experiments.

code_notes.text: The official pseudocode constructs the perpendicular companion, applies q cos plus q-perpendicular sin and the analogous key operation, then takes the standard attention dot product.

agent_input.claim_policy: Use the paper's terms: formulation of relative position encoding, rotary position embedding, sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, and linear self-attention. Attribute every related-work equation to its predecessor paper and distinguish what changed, what remained additive, and which question led to Eq. 11. Keep exact algebra separate from empirical findings and extrapolation claims.

concept_graph.thesis: RoPE encodes absolute position through rotations of query and key representations so that their inner product explicitly contains relative position.

nodes.label: Absolute position encoding
nodes.summary: Learned or sinusoidal position representations are added to token representations.

nodes.label: Relative position encoding
nodes.summary: Prior work introduces relative displacement into self-attention.

nodes.label: Formulation of relative position encoding
nodes.summary: The query-key inner product should be a function of embeddings and m−n.

nodes.label: Rotary position embedding
nodes.summary: Block-diagonal rotations encode position multiplicatively in queries and keys.

nodes.label: Linear self-attention
nodes.summary: The paper emphasizes compatibility with linear self-attention because rotation acts on query and key representations.

edges.teaching_point: The functions receive absolute positions m and n.
The inner product should depend on m−n.
The paper derives a two-dimensional rotary solution and generalizes it.
The positional operation remains on query and key vectors.

unresolved: Equation 16 establishes relative position in the inner product, not a universal guarantee of length extrapolation or downstream performance.

lesson_plan.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
lesson_plan.promise: Reconstruct the history from absolute addition to relative attention, turn that history into Eq. 11's design question, derive the rotary solution, map it to official pseudocode, and keep algebraic properties separate from reported findings.

sections.title: Position information in self-attention
sections.intent: Start from why attention loses order, then explain why the history moves from absolute position vectors toward relative position in the score before stating RoFormer's construction.
sections.question: What information does each generation of position encoding add, and what remains unresolved before RoPE?
sections.learning_goal: Explain why Eq. 11 is the next question produced by the absolute-to-relative lineage.
sections.misconception: Dot-product attention already contains token order without a positional construction.
sections.summary: Content-only self-attention is permutation-equivariant; the paper traces how absolute and relative methods restore order, then asks for absolute query/key phases whose score depends on relative displacement.

sections.title: From absolute addition to relative attention
sections.intent: Follow Eqs. 3–10 through additive and decomposed position terms so RoPE's multiplicative change is a visible answer to a known design thread.
sections.question: What did Transformer, Shaw, Transformer-XL, T5/TUPE/DeBERTa each change in the attention equation?
sections.learning_goal: Use the paper's categories for learned absolute, sinusoidal absolute, and relative position encoding.
sections.misconception: RoPE is another vector added to token embeddings.
sections.summary: The paper contrasts prior additive position encodings with a multiplicative transformation of queries and keys.

sections.title: Formulation of relative position encoding
sections.intent: Turn the desired relative-position dependence into the functional requirement of Eq. 11 before deriving a solution.
sections.question: What functions f_q and f_k satisfy Eq. 11?
sections.learning_goal: Read the requirement that the inner product depend on x_m, x_n, and m−n.
sections.misconception: The derivation starts from a rotation chosen for visual appeal.
sections.summary: Equation 11 states the target first; the two-dimensional solution follows from that functional requirement.

sections.title: Rotary position embedding
sections.intent: Generalize the two-dimensional rotary solution and show how orthogonality converts absolute rotations into the relative identity of Eq. 16.
sections.question: Why does Eq. 16 contain R at n−m, and how is the rotation implemented?
sections.learning_goal: Connect Eqs. 14-16 to the perpendicular-vector code pattern.
sections.misconception: A dense block-diagonal rotation matrix must be materialized.
sections.summary: Query and key receive position-dependent rotations; orthogonality yields a relative rotation in their inner product, implemented with elementwise cosine and sine.

sections.title: Properties and experimental findings
sections.intent: Separate algebraic properties and decay analysis from empirical evidence on translation, pre-training, GLUE, and longer-sequence legal judgment.
sections.question: Which statements are algebraic properties, and which are empirical findings?
sections.learning_goal: Read sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, linear self-attention, Table 1, and Figure 3 at the correct evidence level.
sections.misconception: A 0.2 BLEU difference proves every claimed property.
sections.summary: The derivation supports the relative-position structure; the paper separately reports WMT and pre-training results.

sections.title: What follows from Eq. 16, and what does not
sections.intent: Preserve the paper's own admitted explanatory gaps and avoid turning a relative-position identity into an unconditional extrapolation guarantee.
sections.question: Does relative rotation guarantee arbitrary-context extrapolation?
sections.learning_goal: Keep an exact identity separate from optimization and extrapolation outcomes.
sections.misconception: Relative position in the score is sufficient for universal length generalization.
sections.summary: Equation 16 is exact; performance and extrapolation remain conditional on frequencies, architecture, data, and training.

blocks.eyebrow: Why position needs its own mechanism
blocks.heading: Content similarity alone does not encode token order
paragraphs: Without positional information, self-attention compares token representations but does not by itself distinguish a permutation of those tokens. The original Transformer restores absolute index by adding a position vector. The relative-position line of work asks a different question: can the attention score see how far and in which direction two tokens are separated?

RoFormer treats that history as a design constraint rather than a list of baselines. Its Eq. 11 asks for query and key functions that receive absolute positions m and n while their inner product depends explicitly on m−n. Rotation is derived as an answer after this requirement is stated.

blocks.label: Paper's design question
blocks.question: Can absolute position m and n enter f_q and f_k while their inner product depends on the relative position m−n?
blocks.context: This requirement becomes Eq. 11. The paper also seeks sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, and compatibility with linear self-attention.

columns.label: Inputs to f_q and f_k
columns.question: What representations are constructed?
columns.answer: Functions receive a word embedding and its absolute position.

columns.label: Input to g
columns.question: What should the score retain?
columns.answer: The word embeddings and relative position m−n.

blocks.heading: Why this formulation matters
paragraphs: The paper does not begin by assuming that rotation is the answer. It first states a functional requirement for the query-key inner product, then derives a two-dimensional solution that can be generalized across the hidden dimension.

blocks.title: A paper-by-paper history of what position changes inside attention

stages.intent: Add sinusoidal absolute position before projection.
stages.change: Limitation carried forward: relative displacement is not explicit in the score.

stages.intent: Insert a learned relative-position representation into the compatibility term.
stages.change: Advance: displacement becomes explicit, but it is still added to a content key.

stages.intent: Decompose content–content, content–position, and global bias terms.
stages.change: Advance: relative interactions are explicit, but the score is still an additive decomposition.

stages.intent: Retain, simplify, or disentangle selected content and position interactions.
stages.change: RoFormer's shared diagnosis: each variant still edits terms in an additive decomposition.

stages.intent: Ask for absolute-position functions whose inner product depends on m−n.
stages.change: Design turn: state the relation before selecting the positional operator.

stages.intent: Pair dimensions and apply absolute rotations to query and key.
stages.change: Answer: two multiplicative absolute phases collapse to the relative phase R_{n−m}.

folded.reason: Proof and efficient-realization steps repeat the paired rotation; code mapping and appendix preserve them without thirteen near-duplicate clips.

blocks.title: Pause on the problem each predecessor solved
blocks.intro: The clip now holds each source long enough to read one equation and one limitation. The score remains the anchor; only the role of position changes.
blocks.observation: Sinusoidal encoding adds absolute position; Shaw adds a relative key; Transformer-XL decomposes score terms; later variants edit them; RoPE changes the operator.
blocks.consequence: Eq. 11 becomes the next scientific question: can absolute phases enter q and k while their dot product exposes m−n?

blocks.heading: Additive versus multiplicative
paragraphs: The paper describes prior position encodings as additive and presents RoPE as multiplicative: position acts through a transformation of query and key representations rather than only through a vector added to token content.

blocks.title: Further reading from the paper's context

items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.relation: Sinusoidal absolute position encoding in the original Transformer.

items.title: Self-Attention with Relative Position Representations
items.relation: Relative position representations directly in self-attention.

items.title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context
items.relation: Relative positional attention for longer context recurrence.

items.title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention
Rethinking Attention with Performers
items.relation: Linear self-attention context used in the paper's Performer experiment.

steps.label: Query input
steps.meaning: The query transformation receives embedding x_m and absolute position m.

steps.label: Key input
steps.meaning: The key transformation receives embedding x_n and absolute position n.

steps.label: Required relation
steps.meaning: Their inner product must expose relative position.

blocks.formula: (R_m W^q x_m)^T(R_n W^k x_n) = x_m^T W^{qT} R_{n−m} W^k x_n

steps.label: Query
steps.meaning: Equation 14 applies an absolute-position rotation to the query.

steps.label: Key
steps.meaning: The key receives the corresponding rotation for position n.

steps.label: Inner product
steps.meaning: Orthogonality produces the relative rotation in Eq. 16.

blocks.title: Why the score contains n−m
blocks.intro: The query and key still receive absolute-position rotations R_m and R_n. The relative term appears only when their inner product combines those rotations.
blocks.observation: Because each rotation is orthogonal, (R_m)^T R_n equals R_{n−m}.
blocks.consequence: The attention score depends on relative displacement even though q_m and k_n are constructed with their own absolute positions.

blocks.title: How one d-dimensional rotation becomes many 2D rotations
blocks.intro: The block-diagonal form groups adjacent coordinates, then applies the same absolute position m with a different frequency θ_j to each pair.
blocks.observation: The three arrows rotate by different angles even though they share the same position index.
blocks.consequence: The high-dimensional RoPE construction can be implemented by parallel pairwise negate-and-swap operations rather than a dense matrix multiply.

lines.maps_to: R_m q = q cos(mθ) + q_perp sin(mθ)
ordinary query-key dot product after rotation

blocks.heading: No dense rotary matrix is materialized
paragraphs: The negate-and-swap operation constructs a perpendicular companion for every adjacent coordinate pair. Elementwise cosine and sine then realize the same two-dimensional rotations in parallel.

blocks.question: Why does the paper claim long-term decay?
blocks.setting: Frequency-paired decomposition of the rotary attention score in Eqs. 35–37.
blocks.metric: A distance-dependent bound on the summed rotary contribution.
blocks.evidence: The derivation uses an Abel transformation and Figure 2 illustrates the reported decay. The animation is schematic, not digitized.
blocks.takeaway: The paper links increasing relative distance to reduced alignment across frequency-paired contributions.

blocks.title: From the frequency sum to a decay bound
blocks.intro: The formula stays visible while a point moves along a schematic distance-dependent bound.
blocks.observation: As |m−n| grows, the displayed bound decreases; the clip explicitly labels the curve as schematic.
blocks.consequence: This explains the direction of the paper's analysis without fabricating Figure 2 coordinates.

blocks.question: Is RoFormer uniformly better across GLUE?
blocks.setting: Six GLUE datasets under the paper's reported pre-training setup.
blocks.evidence: Higher on MRPC, STS-B, and QQP; lower on SST-2, QNLI, and MNLI in Table 2.
blocks.takeaway: The result is mixed rather than a universal improvement; signed deltas make that visible.

blocks.question: Does a longer input help on CAIL2019-SCM?
blocks.takeaway: The longer setting is higher, while the sequential training schedule prevents a pure causal attribution to length alone.

blocks.title: Mixed task deltas, then a longer-sequence comparison
blocks.intro: The clip first animates signed RoFormer−BERT deltas for GLUE, then changes the question and metric to CAIL2019-SCM test accuracy.
blocks.observation: GLUE contains positive and negative deltas. On CAIL2019-SCM, RoFormer-1024 is 1.50 points above RoFormer-512.
blocks.consequence: The page preserves both the mixed broad benchmark and the focused long-text result instead of selecting one flattering bar.

blocks.title: Put the exact WMT14 difference on its reported scale
blocks.intro: A non-zero number line preserves the small absolute difference between Transformer-base and RoFormer on one translation setting.
blocks.observation: Table 1 reports 27.3 BLEU for Transformer-base and 27.5 for RoFormer, a difference of +0.2.
blocks.consequence: This supports retained translation quality in the reported experiment; it is not evidence of a large universal gain.

items.label: Language-model pre-training
items.finding: The paper reports that RoFormer converges faster than the BERT baseline in the shown pre-training experiment.

items.label: Performer with RoPE
items.finding: The paper reports rapid convergence and lower loss than Performer without RoPE in the shown experiment.

blocks.heading: Read each piece of evidence at its own level
paragraphs: Equation 16 establishes a relative-position identity. Table 1 provides one exact task comparison. Figure 3 supplies reported training trends. No single result substitutes for the others.

items.label: Exact algebra
items.detail: Equation 16 follows from the rotation construction and orthogonality.

items.label: Frequency schedule
items.detail: Long-term behavior depends on the selected frequencies and how the trained model uses them.

items.label: Optimization
items.detail: Convergence and downstream quality still depend on architecture, data, and training.

items.label: Extrapolation
items.detail: Relative position in the inner product does not by itself guarantee arbitrary-context generalization.

blocks.prompt: Does R_{n−m} mean q_m and k_n do not contain absolute position?
blocks.answer: No. q_m and k_n are individually rotated by m and n. Their inner product combines those rotations into the relative term.

appendix_entries.title: Source provenance
appendix_entries.body: Paper arXiv:2104.09864v5, PDF SHA-256 e9a481…af79. Official RoFormer repository revision dfc678ad…a9b4. Every new media file is hash checked.

appendix_entries.title: Related-work reading order
appendix_entries.body: Start with the Transformer's sinusoidal absolute encoding, then Shaw et al. and Transformer-XL for relative position, DeBERTa for disentangled attention, and Performer for the linear-attention setting used in the RoFormer experiments.

appendix_entries.title: From Eq. 11 to Eq. 16
appendix_entries.body: Equation 11 is the requirement. Equation 12 gives a two-dimensional complex-number solution; Eqs. 14-15 generalize it; Eq. 16 exposes relative position in the inner product.

appendix_entries.title: Formula, animation, and code map
appendix_entries.body: The new Manim source lives in scenes/explainer_pipeline_native.py. It joins Eq. 11, Eqs. 14-16, perpendicular-vector construction, cosine/sine application, and dot-product code without runtime code generation.

appendix_entries.title: Results reading guide
appendix_entries.body: The WMT chart uses exact Table 1 values and marks its truncated axis. Figure 3 statements are kept as the authors' reported trends rather than invented curve samples.

appendix_entries.title: Claim boundary
appendix_entries.body: The derivation establishes relative position inside the query-key score. It does not alone determine optimization quality, downstream performance, or arbitrary-context extrapolation.

manim_compatibility.statement: The formula primitives have observed render evidence on Manim 0.19.0 and 0.20.1. No minimum-version compatibility claim is made.

formulas.title: RoPE relative-position attention identity
formulas.plain_text: (R_m W_q x_m)^T (R_n W_k x_n) = x_m^T W_q^T R_(n-m) W_k x_n

formula_nodes.label: RoPE relative-position attention identity
formula_nodes.expression: (R_m W_q x_m)^T (R_n W_k x_n) = x_m^T W_q^T R_(n-m) W_k x_n

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 3–4 — additive absolute position encoding
formula_nodes.expression: Establish the additive construction used by learned and sinusoidal absolute position encodings.

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 5–10 — additive and decomposed relative position encoding
formula_nodes.expression: Show how prior methods insert relative terms into the expanded attention score.

formula_nodes.label: Eq. 11 — formulation of relative position encoding
formula_nodes.expression: State the functional requirement that the inner product depend on m−n.

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 12–13 — two-dimensional rotary solution
formula_nodes.expression: Solve the functional requirement in the complex plane and make the rotation geometric.

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 14–16 — d-dimensional RoPE and relative identity
formula_nodes.expression: Pair feature dimensions and use orthogonality to obtain R at n−m in the attention score.

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 17–19 — linear self-attention compatibility
formula_nodes.expression: Show how rotation can be inserted without changing the kernelized attention form.

formula_nodes.label: Eqs. 35–37 — long-term decay
formula_nodes.expression: Decompose the rotary score by frequency and bound how it changes with relative distance.

manim_nodes.label: manim.Axes+Arrow+Rotate
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Axes remain fixed while vector rotation is animated around the origin.
limitations: High-dimensional block rotations require a 2D pair decomposition.

manim_nodes.label: manim.MathTex
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Use MathTex for paper equations; isolate semantic substrings before applying Write, Indicate, or TransformMatchingTex.
limitations: Requires a working LaTeX toolchain in the render environment.

manim_nodes.label: manim.Transform
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Source and target objects occupy compatible scene regions.
limitations: Semantic token matching needs TransformMatchingTex or authored grouping.

manim_nodes.label: manim.TransformMatchingTex
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Keep shared mathematical tokens spatially continuous so the reader sees exactly what a method adds, removes, or replaces.
limitations: Requires semantic TeX grouping when equivalent expressions use different tokenization.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.formula_explainer_scene.OperationWalk
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Every operation chip shows semantic operation text plus a non-color origin badge.
limitations: The first renderer lays out a topological sequence rather than arbitrary branches.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.explainer_primitives.MetricBars
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Keep metric, scale, and row identity fixed while transforming only the controlled setting and sourced values.
limitations: Exact numeric rows only; qualitative paper curves require an explicit schematic label.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.explainer_primitives.RoPERelativeRotation
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Preserve vector identity while collapsing two rotations into one relative angle.
limitations: The geometry exposes the exact relative-rotation identity; a numeric equality fixture is still absent.
